caption: Three adjacent suburban houses are involved in fire — the left house has roof flame, the middle is billowing heavy black smoke with no clear visible flame, and the right house has roof flame; a car parked in the driveway between the middle and right houses is fully engulfed in flames.
annotator_notes: Image matches prompt. MUTUAL-HAZARD RULE applied: house_1, house_2, house_3, and car_1 are all already burning, so no may_spread_to between them — propagation has occurred. Instead, mutual worsens edges between adjacent burning entities capture the heat/ember feeding (house_1↔house_2, house_2↔house_3, house_2↔car_1, house_3↔car_1). house_1 and house_3 are NOT adjacent (house_2 separates them) and house_1 and car_1 are similarly non-adjacent, so no mutual edges between those pairs. Self-loop worsens kept on each entity for intrinsic deterioration. Middle house emits smoke_1 as a separate billowing fluid hazard (heavy smoke output without visible flame). No people in scene, so smoke has no harm targets. C13 fix: added worsens self-loop(s) to standing hazard(s) without external targets (smoke_1). Verifier-driven update: ONE merged smoke node fed by ALL burning producers (house_1, house_2, house_3, car_1) via increases_risk_to provenance edges — asymmetric (fire feeds smoke, not vice versa), so increases_risk_to per the mutual-hazard rule, not worsens.
